commentaries.author: Alcuin of York
commentaries.content: sealed with seven seals. That is, either covered by all the fullness of hidden mysteries, or written by the direction of the sevenfold Spirit.

commentaries.author: Andreas of Caesarea
commentaries.content: And I saw in the right hand of him that sat on the throne a scroll written inside and on the back, sealed with seven seals. Andreas of Caesarea sealed with seven seals. this signifies either the fulfillment of the book, which is obscure and unknown to all, or the dispensation of the one who searches the depths of the Spirit of God. 1 Cor 2:10 book written within and without. is the all-wise memory of God. The things written on the outside are easily understood according to the literal meaning, but the things inside symbolizing the spiritual meaning are very hard to comprehend.

commentaries.author: Apringius of Beja
commentaries.content: seven seals. the seven seals are first, the incarnation; second, the nativity; third, the passion; fourth, the death; fifth, the resurrection; sixth, the glory; and seventh, the kingdom. Therefore, Christ fulfilled all these things through his humanity. All Scriptures that were closed and sealed, he opened and unsealed.

commentaries.author: Apringius of Beja
commentaries.content: book. This book is the creation of the entire present world.

commentaries.author: Caesarius of Arles
commentaries.content: book written within and without. Understand it as both testaments! By the outside the old, by the inside the new, which is concealed within the old.

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: And one of the elders said unto me, Weep not; behold, the Lion of the tribe of Judah, the Root of David, hath prevailed to open the book, and to loose its seven seals."

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
A book written within and without. Books were then skins, membranes, or parchments, and when written on both sides part of the writing appeared, though they were rolled up.
Sealed with seven seals, as containing mysteries and secrets of high importance. (Witham)

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Intus et foris, esothen kai opisthen; on the back side. Ver. 12. Accipere virtutem et divinitatem: in the Greek, instead of divinitatem, plouton. In one or two manuscripts of the Marquis de Velez, theoteta.

commentaries.author: Hugh of Saint-Cher
commentaries.content: seven seals. The nativity from the womb, this was signified in the birth of Isaac in Gen 22, The passion was prefigured in the sacrifice of the lamb in Ex. 12 and the goat in Num 7. The resurrection was signified in Sampson who broke through the gates of Gaza in Judges 16. The ascension was signified in the passage in Lev. 1, where one live bird is left and flies away, and in Elijah being caught up in 2 Kgs 2. The sending of the Holy Spirit is in the figure of the fie descending from heaven in 1 Kgs 1. The coming for judgment is signified in Dan 7, and the books were opened.

commentaries.author: Hugh of Saint-Cher
commentaries.content: book. can also be interpreted as the body of Christ which we consume everyday in the sacrament of the alter.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: book within and without. the book is the entire holy Scripture. inside, the spiritual sense, and outside because according to its historical meaning it feeds souls

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: sealed with seven seals. that is, the seven gifts of the Holy Spirit.

commentaries.author: Nerses of Lambron
commentaries.content: in the right hand. Now in his right hand, that is, within his power and knowledge, all mystery of the Deity was enclosed in the books of the prophets. For the Lord does nothing which he does not reveal to his servants the prophets.

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Gorran
commentaries.content:
First is the incarnation Is. 8.
Second is the nativity Is. 7.
Third is the passion Is. 53.
Fourth is the descent into hell Zech 9.
Fifth was the resurrection Hosea 6.
Sixth is the ascension Is. 63.
Seventh is the coming judgment Is. 3.

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Gorran
commentaries.content: And I saw. He uses a copulative conjunction 'and', because what is said here is not a different vision from the preceeding one, but they are joined as one section with another.

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Gorran
commentaries.content: sealed with seven seals. that is, with all obscurity. And because all time is enveloped in seven days, wholeness is rightly signified by the number seven.

commentaries.author: Nicholas of Lyra
commentaries.content: in the right hand. The operative power of God is called metaphorically called His right hand.

commentaries.author: Oecumenius
commentaries.content: book. We recognize the book to be the most wise memory of God.

commentaries.author: Oecumenius
commentaries.content: sealed with seven seals. The number seven, being a perfect number, indicates that the book had been truly and very securely shut and sealed up. The fact that the scroll was closed and sealed indicates, as has been said, the lack of a free approach to God by those whose names were written in the book.

commentaries.author: Paul of Bush
commentaries.content:
book. this is the secret of the ordering of the times of the world.
For no one is capable of this, except the One who has created the periods of times. The seven seals are the secret of the full duration of the world, which is seven thousand years. In every seal there is what will come to pass in that millennium, one by one.

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: This book signifies the Old Testament, which has been given into the hands of our Lord Jesus Christ, who received from the Father judgment.
